text: <par>This record appears to duplicate RN 10732--in fact, there seem to be numerous duplicates for this source.</par>

<par>For the reflex of the Lisu vowel, cf. PLB <reconstruction>*ʔgoŋ²</reconstruction> NECK > Lisu (Northern) <latinform>kɤ⁵⁵tsi⁵⁵</latinform>. (<latinform>-ɤ</latinform> is the corresponding vowel to Fraser’ s -rgh; <latinform>tsi⁵⁵</latinform> is ‘joint’ .). <latinform>-ɤ (-rgh)</latinform> may be the regular reflex of *<latinform>-oŋ</latinform> after velars.</par>
<par>Not in LTSR. Insert forms from DB-PL 544.</par>
<par>A PLB reconstruction with <latinform>*ʔr-</latinform> seems preferable, since <latinform>*ʔw-</latinform> becomes Lahu <latinform>f-</latinform>.</par>

<par>The constriction in the Akha form is unexplained; it is perhaps due to assimilation to the glottal-stop (=zero) initial. This is a Tone *3 etymon in Lolo-Burmese, which is associated with creakiness in any case. The same holds for Achang Longchuan and several Hani forms.</par>
<par>Bradley’ s PL form has little support. See etymon 569 <reconstruction>*pum ⪤ *pwam</reconstruction> SHOULDER.</par>
<par>wrong tone. Check DB’ s justification for <reconstruction>*š</reconstruction> instead of <reconstruction>*s</reconstruction>. Check DB’ s reconstructed tone.</par>

<par>This PLB form set up by Bradley (#633) has only WB for support; elsewhere all Lolo-Burmese forms point to <reconstruction>*-p</reconstruction>. See e.g. the Bisu, Bola, Maru, and Zaiwa cognates. In general it is difficult to distinguish the PLB rhymes <reconstruction>*-ut</reconstruction> and <reconstruction>*-up</reconstruction> based entirely on Loloish (as opposed to Burmish) evidence.</par>
<par>The final consonant in Phunoi <latinform>sùp</latinform> has perhaps assimilated to the preceding vowel.</par>

<par>Note the similarity between this form and 127492 'back of hand' which both have the same first syllable; in the latter it looks like *lak HAND; but that seems inappropriate here. The prefix is very common in Phunoi body part words; in most of those it seems to mean ‘arm’ or ‘hand’ , but not in all (cf. ‘nostril’ ; ‘tendon’ ; etc.).</par>
<par>According to Bradley (1979:304-5), this form coexists with Mpi <latinform>ne²</latinform>; see <xref ref="545">#545</xref>.</par>
<par>The voiceless lateral in Bisu <latinform>hlè</latinform> reflects an <reconstruction>*s-</reconstruction> prefix (cf. Lepcha <latinform>săli</latinform>).</par>
<par>According to Bradley (1979:304-5), this form coexists with Mpi <latinform>tɕhɑ²lɑʔ⁴</latinform>; see <xref ref="1284">#1284 </xref>.</par>
